articles.title: Celebrations erupt on Toronto streets after Spain’s World Cup victory
articles.summary: Fans hit the streets of Toronto in full force Sunday night to celebrate Spain’s World Cup victory over Argentina. On College Street hundreds of people stepped out donning red and yellow flags for a spontaneous fiesta. Outside La Patrona restaurant, fans told CityNews they have been waiting for this moment for 16 years since Spain’s […]

articles.title: Toronto man, 28, charged with 10 drug and gun offences
articles.summary: A Toronto man was arrested and charged Sunday in connection with a drug and gun investigation in the city’s Alexandra Park neighbourhood. According to authorities, 28-year-old Juan De Leon was allegedly driving a car the wrong way on a one-way street. He was pulled over by police officers spotted open alcohol inside the vehicle. During […]

articles.title: Shakira, Madonna and many Muppets take the World Cup stage, along with BTS and Bieber
articles.summary: Madonna, Justin Bieber, BTS and Shakira each brought a bit of one of their hits to the World Cup’s first halftime show in a whirlwind performance Sunday that also squeezed in Burna Boy, Ted Lasso and many Muppets. The show at what’s otherwise known as MetLife Stadium, outside New York, was an attempt to bring Super Bowl vibes to the […]

articles.title: Woman, 91, dead in hospital following Scarborough car crash
articles.summary: A 91-year-old woman has died in hospital following a serious car crash that happened in Scarborough last week. Emergency crews were called to the Port Union area, near Lawrence Avenue East and Charlottetown Boulevard just before 4 p.m. on July 13. The woman was driving a Toyota Corolla on Lawrence Avenue East while a 38-year-old woman […]

articles.title: Woman, 91, dead following 2-vehicle collision in Scarborough last week: Toronto police
articles.summary: Toronto police say a 91-year-old woman is dead following a two-vehicle collision in Scarborough last Monday.

articles.title: Ajax welcomes home Canada Soccer star Derek Cornelius following World Cup run
articles.summary: Soccer star Derek Cornelius was treated to a hero’s welcome in his hometown of Ajax, Ont., after representing Canada in the FIFA World Cup 2026. The town temporarily renamed Pat Bayly Square to Derek Cornelius Square for the homecoming celebration. CBC’s Tyler Cheese spoke to Cornelius and fans about Canada’s historic run at the tournament.

articles.title: 63-year-old Markham man dead after drowning in Lake Muskoka: OPP
articles.summary: Provincial police say a 63-year-old man from Markham, Ont., is dead after he drowned in Lake Muskoka Saturday.

articles.title: OPP recover Markham man’s body after falling off boat into Lake Muskoka
articles.summary: OPP officers say the 63-year-old Markham man fell off a boat near Walker's Point on Lake Muskoka Saturday afternoon.

articles.title: Federal Liberals select Tanveer Shahnawaz as candidate for Beaches-East York byelection
articles.summary: The Liberal Party of Canada has selected Tanveer Shahnawaz as their candidate for the upcoming federal byelection in the Beaches-East York riding.

articles.title: Driver arrested, female pedestrian dead after being struck by vehicle in Mississauga
articles.summary: A woman in her 20s has died after being struck by a driver in a vehicle in Mississauga, according to Peel police.

articles.title: Man arrested, victim identified after fatal stabbing in North York
articles.summary: A Vaughan man has been arrested and a Toronto woman has been identified as the victim after a stabbing in North York, said Toronto police.

articles.title: Toronto's Hope House offers women path out of homelessness. But the city's far from supportive housing goal
articles.summary: A year after St. Felix Centre in downtown Toronto opened Hope House, the rent-geared-to-income supportive housing residence says it's making a difference in the lives of women and children experiencing housing instability, and domestic violence. It's part of the city's plan to create more affordable and long-term housing solutions, as many people are still being turned away from shelters every mon

articles.title: Blue Jays unveil Joe Carter statue outside Rogers Centre honouring World Series championships
articles.summary: A statue honouring the 1992 and 1993 World Series champion Toronto Blue Jays has been unveiled outside of Rogers Centre.

articles.title: Woman dead following stabbing at North York building: Toronto police
articles.summary: Toronto police say a woman is dead after a stabbing at a building in North York Saturday afternoon.

articles.title: Peel police identify 17-year-old brothers as suspects in fatal Brampton shooting
articles.summary: Peel police have identified two teenage brothers as suspects in a fatal shooting in Brampton last week.
